items.abstract_zh: 如今构建更大的语言模型 (LLM) 的趋势在推动自然语言处理性能的同时，也显着增加了推理阶段的延迟。我们观察到，由于向 LLM 添加参数的回报递减，较小的模型可以对大多数查询做出与昂贵的 LLM 相同的预测。基于这一观察，我们设计了 Tabi，这是一个具有多级推理引擎的推理系统，可使用小型模型和可选的 LLM 为要求苛刻的应用程序提供查询服务。 Tabi is optimized for discriminative models (i.e., not generative LLMs) in a serving framework. Tabi 使用校准后的置信度分数来决定是极快地返回小型模型的准确结果还是将其重新路由到法学硕士。 For re-routed queries, it uses attention-based word pruning and weighted ensemble techniques to offset the system overhead and accuracy loss. We implement and evaluate Tabi with multiple tasks and models.我们的结果表明，与最先进的技术相比，Tabi 的平均延迟降低了 21%-40%（具有相当的尾部延迟），同时满足了 LLM 级高精度目标。

items.title: Model Checking Guided Testing for Distributed Systems.
items.abstract:
Distributed systems have become the backbone of cloud computing. Incorrect system designs and implementations can greatly impair the reliability of distributed systems. Although a distributed system design modelled in the formal specification can be verified by formal model checking, it is still challenging to figure out whether its corresponding implementation conforms to the verified specification. An incorrect system implementation can violate its verified specification, and causes intricate bugs.

In this paper, we propose a novel distributed system testing technique, Model checking guided testing ( Mocket ), to fill the gap between the specification and its implementation in a distributed system. Specially, we use the state space generated by formal model checking to guide the testing for the system implementation, and unearth bugs in the target distributed system. To evaluate the feasibility and effectiveness of Mocket, we apply Mocket on three popular distributed systems, and find 3 previously unknown bugs in them.
items.abstract_zh:
分布式系统已成为云计算的支柱。不正确的系统设计和实现会极大地损害分布式系统的可靠性。尽管在形式化规范中建模的分布式系统设计可以通过形式化模型检查来验证，但要弄清楚其相应的实现是否符合已验证的规范仍然具有挑战性。不正确的系统实现可能违反其经过验证的规范，并导致复杂的错误。

在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的分布式系统测试技术，模型检查引导测试（Mocket），以填补分布式系统中的规范与其实现之间的空白。特别地，我们使用形式化模型检查生成的状态空间来指导系统实现的测试，并发现目标分布式系统中的错误。为了评估 Mocket 的可行性和有效性，我们在三个流行的分布式系统上应用了 Mocket，并发现了 3 个以前未知的 bug。

items.title: Hi-Speed DNN Training with Espresso: Unleashing the Full Potential of Gradient Compression with Near-Optimal Usage Strategies.
items.abstract:
Gradient compression (GC) is a promising approach to addressing the communication bottleneck in distributed deep learning (DDL). It saves the communication time, but also incurs additional computation overheads. The training throughput of compression-enabled DDL is determined by the compression strategy , including whether to compress each tensor, the type of compute resources (e.g., CPUs or GPUs) for compression, the communication schemes for compressed tensor, and so on. However, it is challenging to find the optimal compression strategy for applying GC to DDL because of the intricate interactions among tensors. To fully unleash the benefits of GC, two questions must be addressed: 1) How to express any compression strategies and the corresponding interactions among tensors of any DDL training job? 2) How to quickly select a near-optimal compression strategy?

In this paper, we propose Espresso to answer these questions. It first designs a decision tree abstraction to express any compression strategies and develops empirical models to timeline tensor computation, communication, and compression to enable Espresso to derive the intricate interactions among tensors. It then designs a compression decision algorithm that analyzes tensor interactions to eliminate and prioritize strategies and optimally offloads compression from GPUs to CPUs. Experimental evaluations show that Espresso can improve the training throughput over the start-of-the-art compression-enabled system by up to 77% for representative DDL training jobs. Moreover, the computational time needed to select the compression strategy is measured in milliseconds, and the selected strategy is only a few percent from optimal.
items.abstract_zh:
梯度压缩（GC）是解决分布式深度学习（DDL）中通信瓶颈的一种有前景的方法。它节省了通信时间，但也会产生额外的计算开销。启用压缩的DDL的训练吞吐量由压缩策略决定，包括是否压缩每个张量、压缩的计算资源类型（例如CPU或GPU）、压缩张量的通信方案等。然而，由于张量之间复杂的相互作用，找到将 GC 应用于 DDL 的最佳压缩策略具有挑战性。为了充分发挥GC的优势，必须解决两个问题：1）如何表达任何DDL训练作业的压缩策略以及张量之间相应的交互？ 2）如何快速选择接近最优的压缩策略？

在本文中，我们提出 Espresso 来回答这些问题。它首先设计一个决策树抽象来表达任何压缩策略，并开发经验模型来安排张量计算、通信和压缩的时间线，以使 Espresso 能够导出张量之间复杂的相互作用。然后，它设计了一种压缩决策算法，该算法可分析张量交互，以消除策略并确定优先级，并以最佳方式将压缩从 GPU 卸载到 CPU。实验评估表明，对于代表性 DDL 训练作业，Espresso 可以将训练吞吐量比最先进的压缩系统提高高达 77%。此外，选择压缩策略所需的计算时间以毫秒为单位，并且所选策略仅距最佳策略几个百分点。

items.title: Egeria: Efficient DNN Training with Knowledge-Guided Layer Freezing.
items.abstract: Training deep neural networks (DNNs) is time-consuming. While most existing solutions try to overlap/schedule computation and communication for efficient training, this paper goes one step further by skipping computing and communication through DNN layer freezing. Our key insight is that the training progress of internal DNN layers differs significantly, and front layers often become well-trained much earlier than deep layers. To explore this, we first introduce the notion of training plasticity to quantify the training progress of internal DNN layers. Then we design Egeria, a knowledge-guided DNN training system that employs semantic knowledge from a reference model to accurately evaluate individual layers' training plasticity and safely freeze the converged ones, saving their corresponding backward computation and communication. Our reference model is generated on the fly using quantization techniques and runs forward operations asynchronously on available CPUs to minimize the overhead. In addition, Egeria caches the intermediate outputs of the frozen layers with prefetching to further skip the forward computation. Our implementation and testbed experiments with popular vision and language models show that Egeria achieves 19%-43% training speedup w.r.t. the state-of-the-art without sacrificing accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 训练深度神经网络 (DNN) 非常耗时。虽然大多数现有解决方案尝试重叠/安排计算和通信以实现高效训练，但本文更进一步，通过 DNN 层冻结跳过计算和通信。我们的主要见解是，内部 DNN 层的训练进度差异很大，并且前层通常比深层更早得到良好的训练。为了探索这一点，我们首先引入训练可塑性的概念来量化内部 DNN 层的训练进度。然后我们设计了 Egeria，一个知识引导的 DNN 训练系统，它利用参考模型中的语义知识来准确评估各个层的训练可塑性并安全地冻结收敛层，从而节省其相应的后向计算和通信。我们的参考模型是使用量化技术动态生成的，并在可用的 CPU 上异步运行前向操作，以最大限度地减少开销。此外，Egeria 通过预取来缓存冻结层的中间输出，以进一步跳过前向计算。我们对流行视觉和语言模型的实施和测试台实验表明，Egeria 的训练速度提高了 19%-43%。在不牺牲准确性的情况下实现最先进的技术。

items.title: LogGrep: Fast and Cheap Cloud Log Storage by Exploiting both Static and Runtime Patterns.
items.abstract:
In cloud systems, near-line logs are mainly used for debugging, which means they prefer a low query latency for a better user experience, and like any other logs, they also prefer a low overall cost including storage cost to store compressed logs and computation cost to compress logs and execute queries.

This paper proposes LogGrep, the first log compression and query tool that structurizes and organizes log data properly in fine-grained units by exploiting both static and runtime patterns. It first parses logs into variable vectors by exploiting static patterns and then extracts runtime pattern(s) automatically within each variable vector with a novel extraction method. Based on these runtime patterns, LogGrep further decomposes the variable vectors into fine-grained units called "Capsules" and stamps each Capsule with a summary of its values. During the query process, LogGrep can avoid decompressing and scanning Capsules that cannot possibly match the keywords, with the help of the extracted runtime patterns and the Capsule stamps.

We evaluate LogGrep on 21 types of logs from the production environment of Alibaba Cloud, and 16 types of logs from the public datasets. The results show that LogGrep can reduce query latency and overall cost by an order of magnitude compared to state-of-the-art works. Such results have confirmed that exploiting both static and runtime patterns to structurize logs can achieve fast and cheap cloud log storage.
items.abstract_zh:
在云系统中，近线日志主要用于调试，这意味着它们更喜欢低查询延迟以获得更好的用户体验，并且与任何其他日志一样，它们也更喜欢较低的总体成本，包括存储压缩日志的存储成本和压缩日志和执行查询的计算成本。

本文提出了 LogGrep，这是第一个日志压缩和查询工具，它通过利用静态和运行时模式以细粒度单元正确地结构化和组织日志数据。它首先利用静态模式将日志解析为变量向量，然后使用新颖的提取方法在每个变量向量中自动提取运行时模式。基于这些运行时模式，LogGrep 进一步将变量向量分解为称为“Capsule”的细粒度单元，并用其值的摘要标记每个 Capsule。在查询过程中，LogGrep 借助提取的运行时模式和 Capsule 标记，可以避免解压和扫描不可能与关键字匹配的 Capsule。

我们在阿里云生产环境的 21 种日志和公共数据集中的 16 种日志上对 LogGrep 进行了评估。结果表明，与最先进的工作相比，LogGrep 可以将查询延迟和总体成本降低一个数量级。这些结果证实，利用静态和运行时模式来结构化日志可以实现快速且廉价的云日志存储。

items.title: Effective Performance Issue Diagnosis with Value-Assisted Cost Profiling.
items.abstract: Diagnosing performance issues is often difficult, especially when they occur only during some program executions. Profilers can help with performance debugging, but are ineffective when the most costly functions are not the root causes of performance issues. To address this problem, we introduce a new profiling methodology, value-assisted cost profiling , and a tool vProf. Our insight is that capturing the values of variables can greatly help diagnose performance issues. vProf continuously records values while profiling normal and buggy program executions. It identifies anomalies in the values and the functions where they occur to pinpoint the real root causes of performance issues. Using a set of 15 real-world performance bugs in four widely used applications, we show that vProf is effective at diagnosing all of the issues while other state-of-the-art tools diagnose only a few of them. We further use vProf to diagnose longstanding performance issues in these applications that have been unresolved for over four years.
items.abstract_zh: 诊断性能问题通常很困难，尤其是当它们仅在某些程序执行期间发生时。分析器可以帮助进行性能调试，但当最昂贵的功能不是性能问题的根本原因时，分析器就无效了。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了一种新的分析方法、价值辅助成本分析和工具 vProf。我们的见解是，捕获变量的值可以极大地帮助诊断性能问题。 vProf 持续记录值，同时分析正常和有问题的程序执行情况。它可以识别值和函数中出现的异常情况，以查明性能问题的真正根本原因。通过在四个广泛使用的应用程序中使用一组 15 个实际性能错误，我们表明 vProf 可以有效地诊断所有问题，而其他最先进的工具只能诊断其中的少数问题。我们进一步使用 vProf 来诊断这些应用程序中四年多来一直未解决的长期性能问题。

items.title: ALT: Breaking the Wall between Data Layout and Loop Optimizations for Deep Learning Compilation.
items.abstract:
Deep learning models rely on highly optimized tensor libraries for efficient inference on heterogeneous hardware. Current deep compilers typically predetermine layouts of tensors and then optimize loops of operators. However, such unidirectional and one-off workflow strictly separates graph-level optimization and operator-level optimization into different system layers, missing opportunities for unified tuning.

This paper proposes ALT, a deep compiler that performs joint graph-level layout optimization and operator-level loop optimization. ALT provides a generic transformation module to manipulate layouts and loops with easy-to-use primitive functions. ALT further integrates an auto-tuning module that jointly optimizes graph-level data layouts and operator-level loops while guaranteeing efficiency. Experimental results show that ALT significantly outperforms state-of-the-art compilers ( e.g. , Ansor) in terms of both single operator performance ( e.g. , 1.5× speedup on average) and end-to-end inference performance ( e.g. , 1.4× speedup on average).
items.abstract_zh:
深度学习模型依靠高度优化的张量库在异构硬件上进行高效推理。当前的深度编译器通常预先确定张量的布局，然后优化运算符的循环。然而，这种单向、一次性的工作流程将图级优化和算子级优化严格分离到不同的系统层，失去了统一调优的机会。

本文提出了 ALT，一种执行联合图级布局优化和操作员级循环优化的深度编译器。 ALT 提供了一个通用转换模块，通过易于使用的原语函数来操作布局和循环。 ALT还集成了自动调优模块，在保证效率的同时，共同优化图级数据布局和算子级循环。实验结果表明，ALT 在单算子性能（例如平均加速 1.5 倍）和端到端推理性能（例如平均加速 1.4 倍）方面显着优于最先进的编译器（例如 Ansor）。

items.title: DyTIS: A Dynamic Dataset Targeted Index Structure Simultaneously Efficient for Search, Insert, and Scan.
items.abstract: Many datasets in real life are complex and dynamic, that is, their key densities are varied over the whole key space and their key distributions change over time. It is challenging for an index structure to efficiently support all key operations for data management, in particular, search, insert, and scan, for such dynamic datasets. In this paper, we present DyTIS (Dynamic dataset Targeted Index Structure), an index that targets dynamic datasets. DyTIS, though based on the structure of Extendible hashing, leverages the CDF of the key distribution of a dataset, and learns and adjusts its structure as the dataset grows. The key novelty behind DyTIS is to group keys by the natural key order and maintain keys in sorted order in each bucket to support scan operations within a hash index. We also define what we refer to as a dynamic dataset and propose a means to quantify its dynamic characteristics. Our experimental results show that DyTIS provides higher performance than the state-of-the-art learned index for the dynamic datasets considered.
items.abstract_zh: 现实生活中的许多数据集都是复杂且动态的，也就是说，它们的密钥密度在整个密钥空间上是变化的，并且它们的密钥分布随着时间的推移而变化。对于这样的动态数据集，索引结构如何有效地支持数据管理的所有关键操作，特别是搜索、插入和扫描是一项挑战。在本文中，我们提出了 DyTIS（动态数据集目标索引结构），这是一种针对动态数据集的索引。 DyTIS 虽然基于可扩展哈希的结构，但利用数据集密钥分布的 CDF，并随着数据集的增长学习和调整其结构。 DyTIS 背后的关键新颖之处在于按自然键顺序对键进行分组，并按排序顺序维护每个存储桶中的键，以支持哈希索引内的扫描操作。我们还定义了动态数据集，并提出了一种量化其动态特征的方法。我们的实验结果表明，对于所考虑的动态数据集，DyTIS 提供了比最先进的学习索引更高的性能。

items.title: Efficient and Safe I/O Operations for Intermittent Systems.
items.abstract: Task-based intermittent software systems always re-execute peripheral input/output (I/O) operations upon power failures since tasks have all-or-nothing semantics. Re-executed I/O wastes significant time and energy and risks memory inconsistency. This paper presents EaseIO, a new task-based intermittent system that remedies these problems. EaseIO programming interface introduces re-execution semantics for I/O operations to facilitate safe and efficient I/O management for intermittent applications. EaseIO compiler front-end considers the programmer-annotated I/O re-execution semantics to preserve the task's energy efficiency and idem-potency. EaseIO runtime introduces regional privatization to eliminate memory inconsistency caused by idempotence bugs. Our evaluation shows that EaseIO reduces the wasted useful I/O work by up to 3× and total execution time by up to 44% by avoiding 76% of the redundant I/O operations, as compared to the state-of-the-art approaches for intermittent computing. Moreover, for the first time, EaseIO ensures memory consistency during DMA-based I/O operations.
items.abstract_zh: 基于任务的间歇性软件系统总是在电源故障时重新执行外围输入/输出（I/O）操作，因为任务具有全有或全无的语义。重新执行 I/O 会浪费大量时间和精力，并且存在内存不一致的风险。本文提出了 EaseIO，这是一种新的基于任务的间歇系统，可以解决这些问题。 EaseIO编程接口引入了I/O操作的重新执行语义，以促进间歇性应用程序安全高效的I/O管理。 EaseIO 编译器前端考虑程序员注释的 I/O 重新执行语义，以保持任务的能源效率和同效率。 EaseIO运行时引入区域私有化，以消除幂等性错误导致的内存不一致。我们的评估表明，与最先进的间歇计算方法相比，EaseIO 通过避免 76% 的冗余 I/O 操作，将浪费的有用 I/O 工作减少了高达 3 倍，并将总执行时间减少了高达 44%。此外，EaseIO 首次确保了基于 DMA 的 I/O 操作期间的内存一致性。

items.title: DiLOS: Do Not Trade Compatibility for Performance in Memory Disaggregation.
items.abstract: Memory disaggregation has replaced the landscape of dat-acenters by physically separating compute and memory nodes, achieving improved utilization. As early efforts, kernel paging-based approaches offer transparent virtual memory abstraction for remote memory with paging schemes but suffer from expensive page fault handling. This paper revisits the paging-based approaches and challenges their performance in paging schemes. We posit that the overhead of the paging-based approaches is not a fundamental limitation. We propose DiLOS, a new library operating system (LibOS) specialized for paging-based memory disaggregation. We have revamped the page fault handler to get away with the swap cache and incorporated known techniques in our prefetcher, page manager, and communication module for performance optimization. Furthermore, we provide APIs to augment the LibOS with application semantics. We present two app-aware guides, app-aware prefetching and bandwidth-reducing memory allocator in DiLOS. Through extensive evaluation of microbenchmarks and applications, we demonstrate that DiLOS outperforms the state-of-the-art kernel paging-based system (Fastswap) up to 2.24× and a recent user-level system (AIFM) 1.54× on a real-world data analytic workload.
items.abstract_zh: 内存分解通过物理分离计算和内存节点，取代了数据中心的格局，从而提高了利用率。作为早期的努力，基于内核分页的方法通过分页方案为远程内存提供透明的虚拟内存抽象，但受到昂贵的页错误处理的影响。本文重新审视基于分页的方法并挑战它们在分页方案中的性能。我们认为基于分页的方法的开销不是根本限制。我们提出了 DiLOS，这是一种专门用于基于分页的内存分解的新库操作系统 (LibOS)。我们改进了页面错误处理程序以摆脱交换缓存，并将已知技术纳入我们的预取器、页面管理器和通信模块中以实现性能优化。此外，我们还提供 API 来通过应用程序语义来增强 LibOS。我们提出了两个应用程序感知指南，即 DiLOS 中的应用程序感知预取和带宽减少内存分配器。通过对微基准和应用程序的广泛评估，我们证明 DiLOS 在实际数据分析工作负载上的性能优于最先进的基于内核分页的系统 (Fastswap) 高达 2.24 倍，以及最新的用户级系统 (AIFM) 1.54 倍。

items.title: Viper: A Fast Snapshot Isolation Checker.
items.abstract:
Snapshot isolation (SI) is supported by most commercial databases and is widely used by applications. However, checking SI today---given a set of transactions, checking if they obey SI---is either slow or gives up soundness.

We present viper, an SI checker that is sound, complete, and fast. Viper checks black-box databases and hence is transparent to both users and databases. To be fast, viper introduces BC-polygraphs , a new representation of transaction dependencies. A BC-polygraph is acyclic iff transactions are SI, a theorem that we prove. Viper also introduces heuristic pruning , an optimization to accelerate checking SI by leveraging common knowledge of real-world database implementations. Besides vanilla SI, viper supports major SI variants including Strong SI, Generalized SI, and Strong Session SI. Our experiments show that given the same time budget, viper improves over baselines by 15× in the workload sizes being checked.
items.abstract_zh:
大多数商业数据库都支持快照隔离（SI），并且被应用程序广泛使用。然而，今天检查 SI（给定一组交易，检查它们是否遵守 SI）要么很慢，要么放弃稳健性。

我们推出 viper，这是一款健全、完整且快速的 SI 检查器。 Viper 检查黑盒数据库，因此对用户和数据库都是透明的。为了快速起见，viper 引入了 BC-polygraphs，这是一种交易依赖关系的新表示形式。 BC 测谎仪是非循环的，当且仅当交易是 SI，这是我们证明的定理。 Viper 还引入了启发式修剪，这是一种通过利用现实数据库实现的常识来加速检查 SI 的优化。除了普通 SI 之外，viper 还支持主要 SI 变体，包括强 SI、广义 SI 和强会话 SI。我们的实验表明，在时间预算相同的情况下，viper 在检查的工作负载大小方面比基线提高了 15 倍。

items.title: SiloD: A Co-design of Caching and Scheduling for Deep Learning Clusters.
items.abstract:
Deep learning training on cloud platforms usually follows the tradition of the separation of storage and computing. The training executes on a compute cluster equipped with GPUs/TPUs while reading data from a separate cluster hosting the storage service. To alleviate the potential bottleneck, a training cluster usually leverages its local storage as a cache to reduce the remote IO from the storage cluster. However, existing deep learning schedulers do not manage storage resources thus fail to consider the diverse caching effects across different training jobs. This could degrade scheduling quality significantly.

To address this issue, we present SiloD, a scheduling framework that co-designs the cluster scheduler and the cache subsystems for deep learning training. SiloD treats cache and remote IO as first-class resources and can integrate different state-of-the-art deep learning scheduling policies in a unified scheduling framework. To achieve this, SiloD develops an enhanced job performance estimator to help different schedulers to jointly consider the impact of storage and compute resource allocation while preserving their respective scheduling objectives. The SiloD-enhanced performance estimator leverages the unique data access pattern of deep learning training to develop a closed-form analytic model that captures the diverse cache / remote IO requirements from different training jobs. Evaluations show that SiloD improves the average job completion time, cluster utilization, and fairness by up to 7.4x, 2.57x, and 1.89x, respectively, compared to different combinations of cache systems and cluster schedulers where they operate independently.
items.abstract_zh:
云平台上的深度学习训练通常遵循存储与计算分离的传统。训练在配备 GPU/TPU 的计算集群上执行，同时从托管存储服务的单独集群读取数据。为了缓解潜在的瓶颈，训练集群通常利用其本地存储作为缓存，以减少来自存储集群的远程 IO。然而，现有的深度学习调度器不管理存储资源，因此无法考虑不同训练作业的不同缓存效果。这可能会显着降低调度质量。

为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SiloD，一个调度框架，它共同设计了用于深度学习训练的集群调度器和缓存子系统。 SiloD将缓存和远程IO视为一流资源，可以将不同的最先进的深度学习调度策略集成在统一的调度框架中。为了实现这一目标，SiloD 开发了一个增强的作业性能估计器，以帮助不同的调度程序共同考虑存储和计算资源分配的影响，同时保留各自的调度目标。 SiloD 增强型性能估计器利用深度学习训练的独特数据访问模式来开发封闭式分析模型，该模型可以捕获来自不同训练作业的不同缓存/远程 IO 需求。评估表明，与独立运行的缓存系统和集群调度器的不同组合相比，SiloD 将平均作业完成时间、集群利用率和公平性分别提高了 7.4 倍、2.57 倍和 1.89 倍。
